description: Places trapped in a single season or cycling through them in unnatural ways, where time and weather follow their own strange rules.
biomes: Blossoms that never fall and soft rain that comes each afternoon like clockwork. The air is sweet and the green is impossibly fresh.
Golden heat that never breaks and long days that stretch on without relief. The grass is dry and the cicadas never stop singing.
Leaves always falling in shades of amber and rust but the branches never go bare. The air is crisp and smells of wet earth.
Snow that never melts under a sky that never brightens past grey. The cold is patient and thorough, and spring is only a rumor.
Spring, summer, autumn, and winter cycling in hours instead of months. The land cannot settle, and neither can anything living on it.
A single perfect moment preserved in amber light. The leaves hang mid-fall, the rain hovers mid-drop, and nothing changes.
Sun, rain, snow, and wind arriving in rapid succession with no pattern. The sky changes color every few minutes and the ground cannot keep up.
